NKJV.1: After this there was a feast of the Jews, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
NKJV.2: Now there is in Jerusalem by the Sheep Gate a pool, which is called in Hebrew, Bethesda, having five porches.
NKJV.3: In these lay a great multitude of sick people, blind, lame, paralyzed, waiting for the moving of the water.
NKJV.4: For an angel went down at a certain time into the pool and stirred up the water; then whoever stepped in first, after the stirring of the water, was made well of whatever disease he had.
NKJV.5: Now a certain man was there who had an infirmity thirty-eight years.
NKJV.6: When Jesus saw him lying there, and knew that he already had been in that condition a long time, He said to him, “Do you want to be made well?”
NKJV.7: The sick man answered Him, “Sir, I have no man to put me into the pool when the water is stirred up; but while I am coming, another steps down before me.”
NKJV.8: Jesus said to him, “Rise, take up your bed and walk.”
NKJV.9: And immediately the man was made well, took up his bed, and walked. And that day was the Sabbath.
NKJV.10: The Jews therefore said to him who was cured, “It is the Sabbath; it is not lawful for you to carry your bed.”
NKJV.11: He answered them, “He who made me well said to me, ‘Take up your bed and walk.’ ”
NKJV.12: Then they asked him, “Who is the Man who said to you, ‘Take up your bed and walk’?”
NKJV.13: But the one who was healed did not know who it was, for Jesus had withdrawn, a multitude being in that place.
NKJV.14: Afterward Jesus found him in the temple, and said to him, “See, you have been made well.  Sin no more, lest a worse thing come upon you.”
NKJV.15: The man departed and told the Jews that it was Jesus who had made him well.
NKJV.16: For this reason the Jews persecuted Jesus, and sought to kill Him, because He had done these things on the Sabbath.
NKJV.17: But Jesus answered them, “My Father has been working until now, and I have been working.”
NKJV.18: Therefore the Jews sought all the more to kill Him, because He not only broke the Sabbath, but also said that God was His Father, making Himself equal with God.
NKJV.19: Then Jesus answered and said to them, “Most assuredly, I say to you,  the Son can do nothing of Himself, but what He sees the Father do; for whatever He does, the Son also does in like manner.
NKJV.20: For  the Father loves the Son, and  shows Him all things that He Himself does; and He will show Him greater works than these, that you may marvel.
NKJV.21: For as the Father raises the dead and gives life to them, even so the Son gives life to whom He will.
NKJV.22: For the Father judges no one, but  has committed all judgment to the Son,
NKJV.23: that all should honor the Son just as they honor the Father.  He who does not honor the Son does not honor the Father who sent Him.
NKJV.24: “Most assuredly, I say to you,  he who hears My word and believes in Him who sent Me has everlasting life, and shall not come into judgment,  but has passed from death into life.
NKJV.25: Most assuredly, I say to you, the hour is coming, and now is, when  the dead will hear the voice of the Son of God; and those who hear will live.
NKJV.26: For  as the Father has life in Himself, so He has granted the Son to have  life in Himself,
NKJV.27: and  has given Him authority to execute judgment also,  because He is the Son of Man.
NKJV.28: Do not marvel at this; for the hour is coming in which all who are in the graves will  hear His voice
NKJV.29: and come forth— those who have done good, to the resurrection of life, and those who have done evil, to the resurrection of condemnation.
NKJV.30: I can of Myself do nothing. As I hear, I judge; and My judgment is righteous, because  I do not seek My own will but the will of the Father who sent Me.
NKJV.31: “If I bear witness of Myself, My witness is not true.
NKJV.32: There is another who bears witness of Me, and I know that the witness which He witnesses of Me is true.
NKJV.33: You have sent to John,  and he has borne witness to the truth.
NKJV.34: Yet I do not receive testimony from man, but I say these things that you may be saved.
NKJV.35: He was the burning and  shining lamp, and  you were willing for a time to rejoice in his light.
NKJV.36: But  I have a greater witness than John’s; for  the works which the Father has given Me to finish—the very  works that I do—bear witness of Me, that the Father has sent Me.
NKJV.37: And the Father Himself, who sent Me,  has testified of Me. You have neither heard His voice at any time,  nor seen His form.
NKJV.38: But you do not have His word abiding in you, because whom He sent, Him you do not believe.
NKJV.39: You search the Scriptures, for in them you think you have eternal life; and  these are they which testify of Me.
NKJV.40: But you are not willing to come to Me that you may have life.
NKJV.41: “I do not receive honor from men.
NKJV.42: But I know you, that you do not have the love of God in you.
NKJV.43: I have come in My Father’s name, and you do not receive Me; if another comes in his own name, him you will receive.
NKJV.44: How can you believe, who receive honor from one another, and do not seek the honor that comes from the only God?
NKJV.45: Do not think that I shall accuse you to the Father; there is one who accuses you—Moses, in whom you trust.
NKJV.46: For if you believed Moses, you would believe Me;  for he wrote about Me.
NKJV.47: But if you  do not believe his writings, how will you believe My words?”

ESV.1: After this there was a feast of the Jews, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
ESV.2: Now there is in Jerusalem by the Sheep Gate a pool, in Aramaic  called Bethesda,  which has five roofed colonnades.
ESV.3: In these lay a multitude of invalids — blind, lame, and paralyzed.
ESV.5: One man was there who had been an invalid for thirty-eight years.
ESV.6: When Jesus saw him lying there and knew that he had already been there a long time, he said to him, “Do you want to be healed?”
ESV.7: The sick man answered him, “Sir, I have no one to put me into the pool when the water is stirred up, and while I am going another steps down before me.”
ESV.8: Jesus said to him,  “Get up, take up your bed, and walk.”
ESV.9: And at once the man was healed, and he took up his bed and walked. Now that day was the Sabbath.
ESV.10: So the Jews  said to the man who had been healed, “It is the Sabbath, and it is not lawful for you to take up your bed.”
ESV.11: But he answered them, “The man who healed me, that man said to me, ‘Take up your bed, and walk.’”
ESV.12: They asked him, “Who is the man who said to you, ‘Take up your bed and walk’?”
ESV.13: Now the man who had been healed did not know who it was, for Jesus had withdrawn, as there was a crowd in the place.
ESV.14: Afterward Jesus found him in the temple and said to him, “See, you are well! Sin no more, that nothing worse may happen to you.”
ESV.15: The man went away and told the Jews that it was Jesus who had healed him.
ESV.16: And this was why the Jews were persecuting Jesus, because he was doing these things on the Sabbath.
ESV.17: But Jesus answered them, “My Father is working until now, and I am working.”
ESV.18: This was why the Jews were seeking all the more to kill him, because not only was he breaking the Sabbath, but he was even calling God his own Father, making himself equal with God.
ESV.19: So Jesus said to them, “Truly, truly, I say to you, the Son can do nothing of his own accord, but only what he sees the Father doing. For whatever the Father  does, that the Son does likewise.
ESV.20: For the Father loves the Son and shows him all that he himself is doing. And greater works than these will he show him, so that you may marvel.
ESV.21: For as the Father raises the dead and gives them life, so also the Son gives life to whom he will.
ESV.22: For the Father judges no one, but has given all judgment to the Son,
ESV.23: that all may honor the Son, just as they honor the Father. Whoever does not honor the Son does not honor the Father who sent him.
ESV.24: Truly, truly, I say to you, whoever hears my word and believes him who sent me has eternal life. He does not come into judgment, but has passed from death to life.
ESV.25: “Truly, truly, I say to you, an hour is coming, and is now here, when the dead will hear the voice of the Son of God, and those who hear will live.
ESV.26: For as the Father has life in himself, so he has granted the Son also to have life in himself.
ESV.27: And he has given him authority to execute judgment, because he is the Son of Man.
ESV.28: Do not marvel at this, for an hour is coming when all who are in the tombs will hear his voice
ESV.29: and come out, those who have done good to the resurrection of life, and those who have done evil to the resurrection of judgment.
ESV.30: “I can do nothing on my own. As I hear, I judge, and my judgment is just, because I seek not my own will but the will of him who sent me.
ESV.31: If I alone bear witness about myself, my testimony is not true.
ESV.32: There is another who bears witness about me, and I know that the testimony that he bears about me is true.
ESV.33: You sent to John, and he has borne witness to the truth.
ESV.34: Not that the testimony that I receive is from man, but I say these things so that you may be saved.
ESV.35: He was a burning and shining lamp, and you were willing to rejoice for a while in his light.
ESV.36: But the testimony that I have is greater than that of John. For the works that the Father has given me to accomplish, the very works that I am doing, bear witness about me that the Father has sent me.
ESV.37: And the Father who sent me has himself borne witness about me. His voice you have never heard, his form you have never seen,
ESV.38: and you do not have his word abiding in you, for you do not believe the one whom he has sent.
ESV.39: You search the Scriptures because you think that in them you have eternal life; and it is they that bear witness about me,
ESV.40: yet you refuse to come to me that you may have life.
ESV.41: I do not receive glory from people.
ESV.42: But I know that you do not have the love of God within you.
ESV.43: I have come in my Father's name, and you do not receive me. If another comes in his own name, you will receive him.
ESV.44: How can you believe, when you receive glory from one another and do not seek the glory that comes from the only God?
ESV.45: Do not think that I will accuse you to the Father. There is one who accuses you: Moses, on whom you have set your hope.
ESV.46: For if you believed Moses, you would believe me; for he wrote of me.
ESV.47: But if you do not believe his writings, how will you believe my words?”

NASB.1: After these things there was a feast of the Jews, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
NASB.2: Now there is in Jerusalem by the sheep [gate] a pool, which is called in Hebrew Bethesda, having five porticoes.

NASB.4: for an angel of the Lord went down at certain seasons into the pool and stirred up the water; whoever then first, after the stirring up of the water, stepped in was made well from whatever disease with which he was afflicted].
NASB.5: A man was there who had been ill for thirty-eight years.
NASB.6: When Jesus saw him lying [there], and knew that he had already been a long time [in that condition], He said to him, 'Do you wish to get well?'
NASB.7: The sick man answered Him, 'Sir, I have no man to put me into the pool when the water is stirred up, but while I am coming, another steps down before me.'
NASB.8: Jesus said to him, 'Get up, pick up your pallet and walk.'
NASB.9: Immediately the man became well, and picked up his pallet and [began] to walk. Now it was the Sabbath on that day.
NASB.10: So the Jews were saying to the man who was cured, 'It is the Sabbath, and it is not permissible for you to carry your pallet.'
NASB.11: But he answered them, 'He who made me well was the one who said to me, 'Pick up your pallet and walk.''
NASB.12: They asked him, 'Who is the man who said to you, 'Pick up [your pallet] and walk '?'
NASB.13: But the man who was healed did not know who it was, for Jesus had slipped away while there was a crowd in [that] place.
NASB.14: Afterward Jesus found him in the temple and said to him, 'Behold, you have become well; do not sin anymore, so that nothing worse happens to you.'
NASB.15: The man went away, and told the Jews that it was Jesus who had made him well.
NASB.16: For this reason the Jews were persecuting Jesus, because He was doing these things on the Sabbath.
NASB.17: But He answered them, 'My Father is working until now, and I Myself am working.'
NASB.18: For this reason therefore the Jews were seeking all the more to kill Him, because He not only was breaking the Sabbath, but also was calling God His own Father, making Himself equal with God.
NASB.19: Therefore Jesus answered and was saying to them, 'Truly, truly, I say to you, the Son can do nothing of Himself, unless [it is] something He sees the Father doing; for whatever the Father does, these things the Son also does in like manner.
NASB.20: 'For the Father loves the Son, and shows Him all things that He Himself is doing; and [the Father] will show Him greater works than these, so that you will marvel.
NASB.21: 'For just as the Father raises the dead and gives them life, even so the Son also gives life to whom He wishes.
NASB.22: 'For not even the Father judges anyone, but He has given all judgment to the Son,
NASB.23: so that all will honor the Son even as they honor the Father. He who does not honor the Son does not honor the Father who sent Him.
NASB.24: 'Truly, truly, I say to you, he who hears My word, and believes Him who sent Me, has eternal life, and does not come into judgment, but has passed out of death into life.
NASB.25: 'Truly, truly, I say to you, an hour is coming and now is, when the dead will hear the voice of the Son of God, and those who hear will live.
NASB.26: 'For just as the Father has life in Himself, even so He gave to the Son also to have life in Himself;
NASB.27: and He gave Him authority to execute judgment, because He is [the] Son of Man.
NASB.28: 'Do not marvel at this; for an hour is coming, in which all who are in the tombs will hear His voice,
NASB.29: and will come forth; those who did the good [deeds] to a resurrection of life, those who committed the evil [deeds] to a resurrection of judgment.
NASB.30: 'I can do nothing on My own initiative. As I hear, I judge; and My judgment is just, because I do not seek My own will, but the will of Him who sent Me.
NASB.31: 'If I [alone] testify about Myself, My testimony is not true.
NASB.32: 'There is another who testifies of Me, and I know that the testimony which He gives about Me is true.
NASB.33: 'You have sent to John, and he has testified to the truth.
NASB.34: 'But the testimony which I receive is not from man, but I say these things so that you may be saved.
NASB.35: 'He was the lamp that was burning and was shining and you were willing to rejoice for a while in his light.
NASB.36: 'But the testimony which I have is greater than [the testimony of] John; for the works which the Father has given Me to accomplish-- the very works that I do-- testify about Me, that the Father has sent Me.
NASB.37: 'And the Father who sent Me, He has testified of Me. You have neither heard His voice at any time nor seen His form.
NASB.38: 'You do not have His word abiding in you, for you do not believe Him whom He sent.
NASB.39: 'You search the Scriptures because you think that in them you have eternal life; it is these that testify about Me;
NASB.40: and you are unwilling to come to Me so that you may have life.
NASB.41: 'I do not receive glory from men;
NASB.42: but I know you, that you do not have the love of God in yourselves.
NASB.43: 'I have come in My Father's name, and you do not receive Me; if another comes in his own name, you will receive him.
NASB.44: 'How can you believe, when you receive glory from one another and you do not seek the glory that is from the [one and] only God?
NASB.45: 'Do not think that I will accuse you before the Father; the one who accuses you is Moses, in whom you have set your hope.
NASB.46: 'For if you believed Moses, you would believe Me, for he wrote about Me.
NASB.47: 'But if you do not believe his writings, how will you believe My words?'

NLT.1: Afterward Jesus returned to Jerusalem for one of the Jewish holy days.
NLT.2: Inside the city, near the Sheep Gate, was the pool of Bethesda, with five covered porches.
NLT.3: Crowds of sick people — blind, lame, or paralyzed — lay on the porches.
NLT.5: One of the men lying there had been sick for thirty-eight years.
NLT.6: When Jesus saw him and knew he had been ill for a long time, he asked him, “Would you like to get well?”
NLT.7: “I can’t, sir,” the sick man said, “for I have no one to put me into the pool when the water bubbles up. Someone else always gets there ahead of me.”
NLT.8: Jesus told him, “Stand up, pick up your mat, and walk!”
NLT.9: Instantly, the man was healed! He rolled up his sleeping mat and began walking! But this miracle happened on the Sabbath,
NLT.10: so the Jewish leaders objected. They said to the man who was cured, “You can’t work on the Sabbath! The law doesn’t allow you to carry that sleeping mat!”
NLT.11: But he replied, “The man who healed me told me, ‘Pick up your mat and walk.’”
NLT.12: “Who said such a thing as that?” they demanded.
NLT.13: The man didn’t know, for Jesus had disappeared into the crowd.
NLT.14: But afterward Jesus found him in the Temple and told him, “Now you are well; so stop sinning, or something even worse may happen to you.”
NLT.15: Then the man went and told the Jewish leaders that it was Jesus who had healed him.
NLT.16: So the Jewish leaders began harassing Jesus for breaking the Sabbath rules.
NLT.17: But Jesus replied, “My Father is always working, and so am I.”
NLT.18: So the Jewish leaders tried all the harder to find a way to kill him. For he not only broke the Sabbath, he called God his Father, thereby making himself equal with God.
NLT.19: So Jesus explained, “I tell you the truth, the Son can do nothing by himself. He does only what he sees the Father doing. Whatever the Father does, the Son also does.
NLT.20: For the Father loves the Son and shows him everything he is doing. In fact, the Father will show him how to do even greater works than healing this man. Then you will truly be astonished.
NLT.21: For just as the Father gives life to those he raises from the dead, so the Son gives life to anyone he wants.
NLT.22: In addition, the Father judges no one. Instead, he has given the Son absolute authority to judge,
NLT.23: so that everyone will honor the Son, just as they honor the Father. Anyone who does not honor the Son is certainly not honoring the Father who sent him.
NLT.24: “I tell you the truth, those who listen to my message and believe in God who sent me have eternal life. They will never be condemned for their sins, but they have already passed from death into life.
NLT.25: “And I assure you that the time is coming, indeed it’s here now, when the dead will hear my voice — the voice of the Son of God. And those who listen will live.
NLT.26: The Father has life in himself, and he has granted that same life-giving power to his Son.
NLT.27: And he has given him authority to judge everyone because he is the Son of Man.
NLT.28: Don’t be so surprised! Indeed, the time is coming when all the dead in their graves will hear the voice of God’s Son,
NLT.29: and they will rise again. Those who have done good will rise to experience eternal life, and those who have continued in evil will rise to experience judgment.
NLT.30: I can do nothing on my own. I judge as God tells me. Therefore, my judgment is just, because I carry out the will of the one who sent me, not my own will.
NLT.31: “If I were to testify on my own behalf, my testimony would not be valid.
NLT.32: But someone else is also testifying about me, and I assure you that everything he says about me is true.
NLT.33: In fact, you sent investigators to listen to John the Baptist, and his testimony about me was true.
NLT.34: Of course, I have no need of human witnesses, but I say these things so you might be saved.
NLT.35: John was like a burning and shining lamp, and you were excited for a while about his message.
NLT.36: But I have a greater witness than John — my teachings and my miracles. The Father gave me these works to accomplish, and they prove that he sent me.
NLT.37: And the Father who sent me has testified about me himself. You have never heard his voice or seen him face to face,
NLT.38: and you do not have his message in your hearts, because you do not believe me — the one he sent to you.
NLT.39: “You search the Scriptures because you think they give you eternal life. But the Scriptures point to me!
NLT.40: Yet you refuse to come to me to receive this life.
NLT.42: because I know you don’t have God’s love within you.
NLT.43: For I have come to you in my Father’s name, and you have rejected me. Yet if others come in their own name, you gladly welcome them.
NLT.44: No wonder you can’t believe! For you gladly honor each other, but you don’t care about the honor that comes from the one who alone is God.
NLT.45: “Yet it isn’t I who will accuse you before the Father. Moses will accuse you! Yes, Moses, in whom you put your hopes.
NLT.46: If you really believed Moses, you would believe me, because he wrote about me.
NLT.47: But since you don’t believe what he wrote, how will you believe what I say?”

WEB.24: “Most certainly I tell you, he who hears my word, and believes him who sent me, has eternal life, and doesn’t come into judgment, but has passed out of death into life.

WEB.28: Don’t marvel at this, for the hour comes, in which all that are in the tombs will hear his voice,

WEB.31: “If I testify about myself, my witness is not valid.
WEB.32: It is another who testifies about me. I know that the testimony which he testifies about me is true.
WEB.33: You have sent to John, and he has testified to the truth.
WEB.34: But the testimony which I receive is not from man. However, I say these things that you may be saved.

WEB.41: I don’t receive glory from men.

WEB.46: For if you believed Moses, you would believe me; for he wrote about me.
WEB.47: But if you don’t believe his writings, how will you believe my words?”

AMP.1: Later on there was a Jewish feast (festival), and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
AMP.2: Now in Jerusalem, near the Sheep Gate, there is a pool, which is called in Hebrew (Jewish Aramaic) Bethesda, having five porticoes (alcoves, colonnades).
AMP.3: In these porticoes lay a great number of people who were sick, blind, lame, withered, [waiting for the stirring of the water;
AMP.4: for an angel of the Lord went down into the pool at appointed seasons and stirred up the water; the first one to go in after the water was stirred was healed of his disease.]
AMP.5: There was a certain man there who had been ill for thirty-eight years.
AMP.6: When Jesus noticed him lying there [helpless], knowing that he had been in that condition a long time, He said to him, "Do you want to get well?"
AMP.7: The invalid answered, "Sir, I have no one to put me in the pool when the water is stirred up, and while I am coming [to get into it myself], someone else steps down ahead of me."
AMP.8: Jesus said to him, "Get up; pick up your pallet and walk."
AMP.9:
Immediately the man was healed and recovered his strength, and picked up his pallet and walked.
Now that day was the Sabbath.
AMP.10: So the Jews kept saying to the man who had been healed, "It is the Sabbath, and you are not permitted to pick up your pallet [because it is unlawful]."
AMP.11: He answered them, "The Man who healed me and gave me back my strength was the One who said to me, 'Pick up your pallet and walk.'"
AMP.12: They asked him, "Who is the Man who told you, 'Pick up your pallet and walk'?"
AMP.13: Now the man who had been healed did not know who it was, for Jesus had slipped away [unnoticed] since there was a crowd in that place.
AMP.14: Afterward, Jesus found him in the temple and said to him, "See, you are well! Stop sinning or something worse may happen to you."
AMP.15: The man went away and told the Jews that it was Jesus who had made him well.
AMP.16: For this reason the Jews began to persecute Jesus continually because He was doing these things on the Sabbath.
AMP.17: But Jesus answered them, "My Father has been working until now [He has never ceased working], and I too am working."
AMP.18: This made the Jews more determined than ever to kill Him, for not only was He breaking the Sabbath [from their viewpoint], but He was also calling God His own Father, making Himself equal with God.
AMP.19: So Jesus answered them by saying, "I assure you and most solemnly say to you, the Son can do nothing of Himself [of His own accord], unless it is something He sees the Father doing; for whatever things the Father does, the Son [in His turn] also does in the same way.
AMP.20: For the Father dearly loves the Son and shows Him everything that He Himself is doing; and the Father will show Him greater works than these, so that you will be filled with wonder.
AMP.21: Just as the Father raises the dead and gives them life [and allows them to live on], even so the Son also gives life to whom He wishes.
AMP.22: For the Father judges no one, but has given all judgment [that is, the prerogative of judging] to the Son [placing it entirely into His hands],
AMP.23: so that all will give honor (reverence, homage) to the Son just as they give honor to the Father. [In fact] the one who does not honor the Son does not honor the Father who has sent Him.
AMP.24: "I assure you and most solemnly say to you, the person who hears My word [the one who heeds My message], and believes and trusts in Him who sent Me, has (possesses now) eternal life [that is, eternal life actually begins—the believer is transformed], and does not come into judgment and condemnation, but has passed [over] from death into life.
AMP.25: I assure you and most solemnly say to you, a time is coming and is [here] now, when the dead will hear the voice of the Son of God, and those who hear it will live.
AMP.26: For just as the Father has life in Himself [and is self-existent], even so He has given to the Son to have life in Himself [and be self-existent].
AMP.27: And He has given Him authority to execute judgment, because He is a Son of Man [sinless humanity, qualifying Him to sit in judgment over mankind].
AMP.28: Do not be surprised at this; for a time is coming when all those who are in the tombs will hear His voice,
AMP.29: and they will come out—those who did good things [will come out] to a resurrection of [new] life, but those who did evil things [will come out] to a resurrection of judgment [that is, to be sentenced].
AMP.30: "I can do nothing on my own initiative or authority. Just as I hear, I judge; and My judgment is just (fair, righteous, unbiased), because I do not seek My own will, but only the will of Him who sent Me.
AMP.31: "If I alone testify about Myself, My testimony is not valid.
AMP.32: There is another [My Father] who testifies about Me, and I know [without any doubt] that His testimony on My behalf is true and valid.
AMP.33: You have sent [an inquiry] to John [the Baptist] and he has testified [as an eyewitness] to the truth.
AMP.34: But the testimony I receive is not from man [a merely human witness]; but I say these things so that you may be saved [that is, have eternal life].
AMP.35: John was the lamp that kept on burning and shining [to show you the way], and you were willing for a while to rejoice in his light.
AMP.36: But the testimony which I have is far greater than the testimony of John; for the works that the Father has given Me to finish—the very same works [that is, the miracles and proofs of My deity] that I am [now] doing—testify about Me, [by providing evidence] that the Father has sent Me.
AMP.37: And the Father who sent Me has Himself testified about Me. You have never heard His voice nor seen His form [His majesty and greatness—what He is like].
AMP.38: You do not have His word (Scripture) abiding in you [actually living in your hearts and minds], because you do not believe in Him whom He has sent.
AMP.39: You search and keep on searching and examining the Scriptures because you think that in them you have eternal life; and yet it is those [very Scriptures] that testify about Me;
AMP.40: and still you are unwilling to come to Me so that you may have life.
AMP.41: I do not receive glory and approval from men;
AMP.42: but I know you and recognize that you do not have the love of God in yourselves.
AMP.43: I have come in My Father's name and with His power, and you do not receive Me [because your minds are closed]; but if another comes in his own name and with no authority or power except his own, you will receive him and give your approval to an imposter.
AMP.44: How can you believe [in Me], when you [seek and] receive glory and approval from one another, and yet you do not seek the glory and approval which comes from the one and only God?
AMP.45: Do not think that I [am the One who] will accuse you before the Father. There [already] is one who accuses you: Moses, [the very one] in whom you have placed your hope [for salvation].
AMP.46: For if you believed and relied on [the Scriptures written by] Moses, you would believe Me, for he wrote about Me [personally].
AMP.47: But if you do not believe his writings, how will you believe My words?"

MSG.1: Soon another Feast came around and Jesus was back in Jerusalem.
MSG.2: Near the Sheep Gate in Jerusalem there was a pool, in Hebrew called Bethesda, with five alcoves.
MSG.3: Hundreds of sick people—blind, crippled, paralyzed—were in these alcoves.
MSG.5: One man had been an invalid there for thirty-eight years.
MSG.6: When Jesus saw him stretched out by the pool and knew how long he had been there, he said, "Do you want to get well?"
MSG.7: The sick man said, "Sir, when the water is stirred, I don't have anybody to put me in the pool. By the time I get there, somebody else is already in."
MSG.8: Jesus said, "Get up, take your bedroll, start walking."
MSG.9: The man was healed on the spot. He picked up his bedroll and walked off. That day happened to be the Sabbath.
MSG.10: The Jews stopped the healed man and said, "It's the Sabbath. You can't carry your bedroll around. It's against the rules."
MSG.11: But he told them, "The man who made me well told me to. He said, 'Take your bedroll and start walking.'"
MSG.12: They asked, "Who gave you the order to take it up and start walking?"
MSG.13: But the healed man didn't know, for Jesus had slipped away into the crowd.
MSG.14: A little later Jesus found him in the Temple and said, "You look wonderful! You're well! Don't return to a sinning life or something worse might happen."
MSG.15: The man went back and told the Jews that it was Jesus who had made him well.
MSG.16: That is why the Jews were out to get Jesus—because he did this kind of thing on the Sabbath.
MSG.17: But Jesus defended himself. "My Father is working straight through, even on the Sabbath. So am I."
MSG.18: That really set them off. The Jews were now not only out to expose him; they were out to kill him. Not only was he breaking the Sabbath, but he was calling God his own Father, putting himself on a level with God.
MSG.19: So Jesus explained himself at length. "I'm telling you this straight. The Son can't independently do a thing, only what he sees the Father doing. What the Father does, the Son does.
MSG.20: The Father loves the Son and includes him in everything he is doing. "But you haven't seen the half of it yet,
MSG.21: for in the same way that the Father raises the dead and creates life, so does the Son. The Son gives life to anyone he chooses.
MSG.22: Neither he nor the Father shuts anyone out. The Father handed all authority to judge over to the Son
MSG.23: so that the Son will be honored equally with the Father. Anyone who dishonors the Son, dishonors the Father, for it was the Father's decision to put the Son in the place of honor.
MSG.24: "It's urgent that you listen carefully to this: Anyone here who believes what I am saying right now and aligns himself with the Father, who has in fact put me in charge, has at this very moment the real, lasting life and is no longer condemned to be an outsider. This person has taken a giant step from the world of the dead to the world of the living.
MSG.25: "It's urgent that you get this right: The time has arrived—I mean right now!—when dead men and women will hear the voice of the Son of God and, hearing, will come alive.
MSG.26: Just as the Father has life in himself, he has conferred on the Son life in himself.
MSG.27: And he has given him the authority, simply because he is the Son of Man, to decide and carry out matters of Judgment.
MSG.28: "Don't act so surprised at all this. The time is coming when everyone dead and buried will hear his voice.
MSG.29: Those who have lived the right way will walk out into a resurrection Life; those who have lived the wrong way, into a resurrection Judgment.
MSG.30: "I can't do a solitary thing on my own: I listen, then I decide. You can trust my decision because I'm not out to get my own way but only to carry out orders.
MSG.31: If I were simply speaking on my own account, it would be an empty, self-serving witness.
MSG.32: But an independent witness confirms me, the most reliable Witness of all.
MSG.33: Furthermore, you all saw and heard John, and he gave expert and reliable testimony about me, didn't he?
MSG.34: "But my purpose is not to get your vote, and not to appeal to mere human testimony. I'm speaking to you this way so that you will be saved.
MSG.35: John was a torch, blazing and bright, and you were glad enough to dance for an hour or so in his bright light.
MSG.36: But the witness that really confirms me far exceeds John's witness. It's the work the Father gave me to complete. These very tasks, as I go about completing them, confirm that the Father, in fact, sent me.
MSG.37: The Father who sent me, confirmed me. And you missed it. You never heard his voice, you never saw his appearance.
MSG.38: There is nothing left in your memory of his Message because you do not take his Messenger seriously.
MSG.39: "You have your heads in your Bibles constantly because you think you'll find eternal life there. But you miss the forest for the trees. These Scriptures are all about me!
MSG.40: And here I am, standing right before you, and you aren't willing to receive from me the life you say you want.
MSG.42: And do you know why? Because I know you and your crowds. I know that love, especially God's love, is not on your working agenda.
MSG.43: I came with the authority of my Father, and you either dismiss me or avoid me. If another came, acting self-important, you would welcome him with open arms.
MSG.44: How do you expect to get anywhere with God when you spend all your time jockeying for position with each other, ranking your rivals and ignoring God?
MSG.45: "But don't think I'm going to accuse you before my Father. Moses, in whom you put so much stock, is your accuser.
MSG.46: If you believed, really believed, what Moses said, you would believe me. He wrote of me.
MSG.47: If you won't take seriously what he wrote, how can I expect you to take seriously what I speak?"

NIV.1:
The Healing at the Pool
Some time later, Jesus went up to Jerusalem for a feast of the Jews.
NIV.2: Now there is in Jerusalem near the Sheep Gate a pool, which in Aramaic is called Bethesda and which is surrounded by five covered colonnades.
NIV.3: Here a great number of disabled people used to lie—the blind, the lame, the paralyzed.
NIV.5: One who was there had been an invalid for thirty-eight years.
NIV.6: When Jesus saw him lying there and learned that he had been in this condition for a long time, he asked him, “Do you want to get well?”
NIV.7: “Sir,” the invalid replied, “I have no one to help me into the pool when the water is stirred. While I am trying to get in, someone else goes down ahead of me.”
NIV.8: Then Jesus said to him, “Get up! Pick up your mat and walk.”
NIV.9:
At once the man was cured; he picked up his mat and walked.
The day on which this took place was a Sabbath,
NIV.10: and so the Jews said to the man who had been healed, “It is the Sabbath; the law forbids you to carry your mat.”
NIV.11: But he replied, “The man who made me well said to me, ‘Pick up your mat and walk.’ ”
NIV.12: So they asked him, “Who is this fellow who told you to pick it up and walk?”
NIV.13: The man who was healed had no idea who it was, for Jesus had slipped away into the crowd that was there.
NIV.14: Later Jesus found him at the temple and said to him, “See, you are well again. Stop sinning or something worse may happen to you.”
NIV.15: The man went away and told the Jews that it was Jesus who had made him well.
NIV.16:
Life Through the Son
So, because Jesus was doing these things on the Sabbath, the Jews persecuted him.
NIV.17: Jesus said to them, “My Father is always at his work to this very day, and I, too, am working.”
NIV.18: For this reason the Jews tried all the harder to kill him; not only was he breaking the Sabbath, but he was even calling God his own Father, making himself equal with God.
NIV.19: Jesus gave them this answer: “I tell you the truth, the Son can do nothing by himself; he can do only what he sees his Father doing, because whatever the Father does the Son also does.
NIV.20: For the Father loves the Son and shows him all he does. Yes, to your amazement he will show him even greater things than these.
NIV.21: For just as the Father raises the dead and gives them life, even so the Son gives life to whom he is pleased to give it.
NIV.22: Moreover, the Father judges no one, but has entrusted all judgment to the Son,
NIV.23: that all may honor the Son just as they honor the Father. He who does not honor the Son does not honor the Father, who sent him.
NIV.24: “I tell you the truth, whoever hears my word and believes him who sent me has eternal life and will not be condemned; he has crossed over from death to life.
NIV.25: I tell you the truth, a time is coming and has now come when the dead will hear the voice of the Son of God and those who hear will live.
NIV.26: For as the Father has life in himself, so he has granted the Son to have life in himself.
NIV.27: And he has given him authority to judge because he is the Son of Man.
NIV.28: “Do not be amazed at this, for a time is coming when all who are in their graves will hear his voice
NIV.29: and come out—those who have done good will rise to live, and those who have done evil will rise to be condemned.
NIV.30: By myself I can do nothing; I judge only as I hear, and my judgment is just, for I seek not to please myself but him who sent me.
NIV.31:
Testimonies About Jesus
“If I testify about myself, my testimony is not valid.
NIV.32: There is another who testifies in my favor, and I know that his testimony about me is valid.
NIV.33: “You have sent to John and he has testified to the truth.
NIV.34: Not that I accept human testimony; but I mention it that you may be saved.
NIV.35: John was a lamp that burned and gave light, and you chose for a time to enjoy his light.
NIV.36: “I have testimony weightier than that of John. For the very work that the Father has given me to finish, and which I am doing, testifies that the Father has sent me.
NIV.37: And the Father who sent me has himself testified concerning me. You have never heard his voice nor seen his form,
NIV.38: nor does his word dwell in you, for you do not believe the one he sent.
NIV.39: You diligently study the Scriptures because you think that by them you possess eternal life. These are the Scriptures that testify about me,
NIV.40: yet you refuse to come to me to have life.
NIV.41: “I do not accept praise from men,
NIV.42: but I know you. I know that you do not have the love of God in your hearts.
NIV.43: I have come in my Father’s name, and you do not accept me; but if someone else comes in his own name, you will accept him.
NIV.44: How can you believe if you accept praise from one another, yet make no effort to obtain the praise that comes from the only God?
NIV.45: “But do not think I will accuse you before the Father. Your accuser is Moses, on whom your hopes are set.
NIV.46: If you believed Moses, you would believe me, for he wrote about me.
NIV.47: But since you do not believe what he wrote, how are you going to believe what I say?”

NET.28: “Do not be amazed at this, because a time is coming when all who are in the tombs will hear his voice
NET.29: and will come out — the ones who have done what is good to the resurrection resulting in life, and the ones who have done what is evil to the resurrection resulting in condemnation.
NET.30: I can do nothing on my own initiative. Just as I hear, I judge, and my judgment is just, because I do not seek my own will, but the will of the one who sent me.
NET.31: “If I testify about myself, my testimony is not true.
NET.32: There is another who testifies about me, and I know the testimony he testifies about me is true.
NET.33: You have sent to John, and he has testified to the truth.
NET.34: (I do not accept human testimony, but I say this so that you may be saved.)
NET.35: He was a lamp that was burning and shining, and you wanted to rejoice greatly for a short time in his light.
NET.36: “But I have a testimony greater than that from John. For the deeds that the Father has assigned me to complete — the deeds I am now doing — testify about me that the Father has sent me.
NET.37: And the Father who sent me has himself testified about me. You people have never heard his voice nor seen his form at any time,
NET.38: nor do you have his word residing in you, because you do not believe the one whom he sent.
NET.39: You study the scriptures thoroughly because you think in them you possess eternal life, and it is these same scriptures that testify about me,
NET.40: but you are not willing to come to me so that you may have life.
NET.41: “I do not accept praise from people,
NET.42: but I know you, that you do not have the love of God within you.
NET.43: I have come in my Father’s name, and you do not accept me. If someone else comes in his own name, you will accept him.
NET.44: How can you believe, if you accept praise from one another and don’t seek the praise that comes from the only God?
NET.45: “Do not suppose that I will accuse you before the Father. The one who accuses you is Moses, in whom you have placed your hope.
NET.46: If you believed Moses, you would believe me, because he wrote about me.
NET.47: But if you do not believe what Moses wrote, how will you believe my words?”
